claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period; Child has started an apprenticeship in England
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let me start with the basics:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex, who is 17 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I'm assessing eligibility for your son Alex, who is 17 years old.

Since Alex is 17, I need to understand his current situation, as there are specific rules for children aged 16 and over.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

(This would include things like A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQ Level 3 or below, or other approved non-advanced education or training courses.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's not in sixth form or anything like that, but he has started an apprenticeship recently.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. An apprenticeship can count as approved training for Child Benefit purposes.

To determine if Alex's apprenticeship qualifies, I need to know:

**Is Alex's apprenticeship unpaid, or does he receive any payment/wage for it?**

(Child Benefit can continue for young people aged 16-19 in approved unpaid education or training that doesn't include employment.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm not entirely sure about the exact details, but I know he does get paid for it - it's a proper apprenticeship with a wage.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. This is an important detail.

Unfortunately, **paid apprenticeships do not qualify as approved education or training for Child Benefit purposes**. Child Benefit for 16-19 year olds only continues if they are in:
- Approved **unpaid** education or training (up to 20 hours per week), or
- Full-time non-advanced education (like A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQ Level 3 or below)

Since Alex is 17 and in a paid apprenticeship, this counts as employment rather than qualifying education or training.

Let me confirm one more detail to finalize the assessment:

**When did Alex turn 16, and when did he start this paid apprenticeship?**

(Child Benefit can continue until the 31st August after a child turns 16, or until they leave approved education/training if that's later.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He turned 16 last year, so that was well over a year ago now. And he started the apprenticeship a few months back.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility.

Since Alex turned 16 over a year ago (which means the 31st August deadline after his 16th birthday has passed), and he is now in a paid apprenticeship rather than approved unpaid education or training, **you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:
- Child Benefit automatically stops on 31st August following a child's 16th birthday, unless they continue in approved education or training
- For 16-19 year olds, only **unpaid** education or training (up to 20 hours per week) or full-time non-advanced education qualifies
- Paid apprenticeships count as employment and do not qualify as approved education or training for Child Benefit purposes

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry that the outcome isn't what you were hoping for. If Alex's circumstances change in the future - for example, if he leaves the apprenticeship to enter full-time education - you could potentially become eligible again, though this would need to happen before he turns 20.

Is there anything else you'd like me to clarify about this decision?

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and turned 16 over a year ago. Child Benefit automatically stops on 31st August following a child's 16th birthday unless the child continues in approved education or training. Alex is currently in a paid apprenticeship. For children aged 16-19, Child Benefit only continues if they are in approved unpaid education or training (up to 20 hours per week) or full-time non-advanced education. Paid apprenticeships count as employment and do not qualify as approved education or training for Child Benefit purposes. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 17 years old and in a paid apprenticeship. As Alex turned 16 over a year ago and is now in paid employment (apprenticeship), which does not qualify as approved education or training, Child Benefit eligibility has ended.

Child Benefit automatically stops on 31st August following a child's 16th birthday unless the child continues in approved education or training. Alex is currently in a paid apprenticeship. For children aged 16-19, Child Benefit only continues if they are in approved unpaid education or training (up to 20 hours per week) or full-time non-advanced education. Paid apprenticeships count as employment and do not qualify as approved education or training for Child Benefit purposes. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 17 years old and in a paid apprenticeship. As Alex turned 16 over a year ago and is now in paid employment (apprenticeship), which does not qualify as approved education or training, Child Benefit eligibility has ended.
